channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 2025 will be the year of the CIO and CMO collaboration
item.description: Same executive board, different roles. Historically, that’s been how CIOs and CMOs work and operate across a company. But as we close out 2024, this siloed approach must come to an end.
item.dc:creator: Keith Turco, Fast Company

item.title: Consumer watchdog to oversee digital wallets, payment apps
item.description: Large companies that offer digital wallets and payment apps will now be overseen by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB). The CFPB finalized a rule Thursday to bring companies handling more than 50 million transactions a year under its supervision.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Eric Schmidt on Henry Kissinger’s surprising warning to the world on AI
item.description: Humans are only just beginning to reckon with the implications of sharing our planet with synthetic entities more intelligent than ourselves.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Apple Pay, other tech firms come under CFPB regulatory oversight
item.description: CFPB can already take action against companies that break the law, the new rule would allow the CFPB to regularly supervise the large digital-wallet and payments firms and their practices.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Bitcoin is at the doorstep of $100,000 as post-election rally rolls on
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin topped $98,000 for the first time Thursday, extending a streak of record after record highs since the U.S. presidential election. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: AI-powered dog robot sniffs out invasive fire ants
item.description: Fueled by artificial intelligence, CyberDog the robotic dog is designed specifically locate fire ant nests, which are endangering ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Rise of AI Agents: Transforming Business and Work
item.description: AI agents software programs, business transformation, automation, job reshaping
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What underscores, and undermines, a successful CIO-CEO collaboration
item.description: The worldwide push to digital has quickly promoted the IT department from its back-room origins to the upper reaches within any company or organization.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot incorpora nuevos agentes de IA y automatiza tareas para mejorar flujos de trabajo
item.description: El gigante de Redmond, aprovechando el altavoz de su cita anual Microsoft Ignite 2024, ha anunciado nuevas funcionalidades en su cartera de soluciones.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: US regulators seek to break up Google, forcing Chrome sale as part of monopoly punishment
item.description: U.S. regulators want a federal judge to break up Google to prevent the company from continuing to squash competition through its dominant search engine after a court found it had maintained an abusive monopoly over the past decade.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks that were once the entertainment giant's star performers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast is spinning off into a new company many of the cable television networks that were once at the entertainment giant's heart as consumers increasingly swap out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US ahead in AI innovation, easily surpassing China in Stanford's new ranking
item.description: The U.S. leads the world in developing artificial intelligence technology, surpassing China in research and other important measures of AI innovation, according to a newly released Stanford University index.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The U.S. Space Force Can Modernize at a Smarter Pace
item.description: The U.S. Space Force can modernize its technology by prioritizing data and IT modernization, enabling small businesses and non-traditional vendors to innovate and compete in the space industry.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: Whoever ends up owning Chrome, we could use a new browser war
item.description: Do you have a favorite web browser? I used to: It was called Flock, had an emphasis on social networking and sharing, and died in 2011. Nothing I’ve used since has warmed my heart quite so much. On Monday, Bloomberg’s Leah Nylen and Josh Sisco reported that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Chatbot's disturbing message
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: GSA listening more to customers to improve IAE platforms
item.description: Memi Whitehead, the deputy assistant commissioner for GSA’s Integrated Award Environment, said new tools aim to improve its users’ experiences. The post GSA listening more to customers to improve IAE platforms first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft Unveils Enhanced Copilot Studio Features at Ignite Conference
item.description: Microsoft expands AI agent capabilities, improves accuracy, and adds new features to Copilot Studio at the Ignite conference.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: An Android-ChromeOS merger might actually make sense now
item.description: Stop me if you’ve heard this one: Google’s got two primary user-facing platforms, Android and ChromeOS — and, if the latest rumors are to be believed, the company may be on the brink of bringing ’em together and combining ’em into a single, streamlined entity.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IBM deal brings AMD Instinct accelerators to IBM Cloud
item.description: IBM is expanding its AI accelerator options for enterprise users of its cloud service. AMD Instinct MI300X accelerators are now available as a service on IBM Cloud, the vendors announced.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sweeping VA reform bill with pay flexibility for health care workers passes House
item.description: Lawmakers axed language in the House-passed Dole Act that set higher standards for the VA to resume the rollout of its troubled new Electronic Health Record. The post Sweeping VA reform bill with pay flexibility for health care workers passes House first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A beautiful machine: one week with an M4 Pro MacBook Pro
item.description: The new MacBook Pro is everything you expect: faster, better and more capable than before. While you can say that about every new Mac, the move to Apple Silicon means Apple can introduce vastly improved systems almost every year — something that was not always possible before.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft’s Windows 365 Link is a thin client device for shared workspaces
item.description: Microsoft will start selling a thin client device that lets workers boot directly to Windows 365 “in seconds,” the company announced on Tuesday. Windows 365 Link will cost $349 when it launches next April, but businesses can contact their Microsoft account team and request a private preview.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to transform your architecture review board
item.description: I recall my angst about my first visit to an architecture review board (ARB), where a team of enterprise architects reviewed plans for new systems and application architectures.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Company affiliated with Alex Jones seeks to disqualify The Onion's auction bid on Infowars
item.description: A company affiliated with conspiracy theorist Alex Jones asked a federal judge on Monday to disqualify a bid by the satirical news outlet The Onion to buy Jones' Infowars at a bankruptcy auction, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, Fox 5
